mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, triggering a positive S&P 500 return response over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of real personal savings and housing starts indicates synchronized household balance sheet repair and durable goods demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects pricing power and margin expansion rather than demand saturation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during periods where CPIAUCSL simultaneously rises above 3.0%, indicating demand-pull inflation eroding margins.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL crosses above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, the market prices in a temporary inflation overshoot that will be tolerated by accommodative policy, generating a short-term positive S&P 500 return over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when the trigger condition is met, or the signal fails when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP directly improves corporate profit expectations, producing a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings growth catches up to macroeconomic data.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls rise above 150,000 in a 3-month window alongside a declining unemployment rate, it confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained expansion, a divergence where industrial production rises while GDP growth decelerates signals inventory drawdowns and pricing power, triggering a short-term positive S&P 500 return over 5 trading days as margin expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO and GDPC1 both rise simultaneously, or the signal fails when INDPRO exceeds 105.0, indicating demand saturation rather than supply tightness.
